---
title: "FLORA Converter — Notebook Test"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(httr2)
library(jsonlite)
library(FReD)
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(writexl)

```

## Load the converter

```{r}
source("flora_converter.R")
```

# load data convert
```{r}

data <- csv_to_flora_json("flora.csv")
df   <- flora_json_to_df(data)
df_nona <- df |> filter(!is.na(doi_o))

rand_doi <- sample(df$doi_o, 50)
df_rand50 <- df %>% filter (doi_o %in% rand_doi) # selecting fifty random rows
df_rand50 <- df_rand50 |>
              mutate(across(where(is.character), \(x) iconv(x, to = "UTF-8", sub = "byte")))

cat("Total original papers:", nrow(df_rand50))

# write_xlsx(df_rand50, "df_rand50.xlsx")

```


# process
1. convert json back to data frame
2. clean up so that only non na doi_o and value in either doi_r or url_r
3. new csv file for output
4. for each unique doi_o generate row with reference, outcome, outcome quote and outcome source columns, column with message. If multiple doi_r should include all in message.



#  Some kinda function
```{r}

# ── Step 1: Count replications per original study
replication_counts <- df_rand50 |>
                    group_by(doi_o) |>
                    summarise(n_replications = n(), .groups = "drop")


# ── Step 2: Clean data and join counts ─────────────────────────────────────────

flora_clean <- df_rand50 |>
                filter(!is.na(doi_o)) |>
                select(
                  title_o, doi_o, journal_o,
                  title_r, doi_r, journal_r,
                  apa_ref_r,
                  outcome, outcome_quote, outcome_quote_source
                ) |>
                left_join(replication_counts, by = "doi_o")




# ── Step 3: Count replications per original study ──────────────────────────────

replication_counts <- df_rand50 |>
  group_by(doi_o) |>
  summarise(n_replications = n(), .groups = "drop")

# ── Step 4: Clean data and join counts ─────────────────────────────────────────

flora_clean <- df_rand50 |>
  filter(!is.na(doi_o)) |>
  select(
    title_o, doi_o, journal_o,
    title_r, doi_r, journal_r,
    apa_ref_r,
    outcome, outcome_quote, outcome_quote_source
  ) |>
  left_join(replication_counts, by = "doi_o")

# ── Step 5: Define comment generator function ───────

generate_comment <- function(doi_o, doi_r, n_replications, apa_ref_r,
                             outcome, outcome_quote, outcome_quote_source, ...) {

              # Build outcome quote block only if it exists
              quote_block <- if (!is.na(outcome_quote) &
                                 nchar(trimws(outcome_quote)) > 0) {
                glue("Outcome quote: {outcome_quote}")
              } else {
                "Outcome quote:"
              }
            
              # Build quote source block only if it exists
              source_block <- if (!is.na(outcome_quote_source) &
                                  nchar(trimws(outcome_quote_source)) > 0) {
                glue("Quote source: {outcome_quote_source}")
              } else {
                'Quote source: "Does not appear"'
              }
            
              # Build the full comment
              glue(
                "According to data from the FORRT Library of Reproduction and Replication ",
                "Attempts (FLoRA) there are {n_replications} replication attempt(s) of a ",
                "finding from this study. Future research may learn from replications and ",
                "build on them. Manual inspection of these articles may help to assess what ",
                "led to the described outcome.\n\n",
                "Replication article reference: {apa_ref_r}\n",
                "Outcome: {outcome}\n",
                "{quote_block}\n",
                "{source_block}\n\n",
                "FLoRA database is available online: https://dx.doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/9r62x\n\n",
                "We describe why and how we submitted these automated comments here: ",
                "https://github.com/forrtproject/flora-pubpeer/blob/main/README.md\n\n",
                "FLoRA Notify Team"
              )
      }

# ── Step 6: Generate comments for all studies ──────────────────────────────────

flora_with_comments <- flora_clean |>
  mutate(
    comment_text = pmap_chr(
      list(doi_o, doi_r, n_replications, apa_ref_r,
           outcome, outcome_quote, outcome_quote_source),
      generate_comment
    )
  )

# ── Step 5: Spot check ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

flora_with_comments |> select(doi_o, comment_text) |> head(3)

# flora_with_comments$comment_text[1]


# ── Step 6: Save outputs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Full dataset with all columns
# write_csv(flora_with_comments, "pubpeer_comments.csv")
# write_xlsx(flora_with_comments, "pubpeer_comments.xlsx")






```

```{r}
# Keeping bits and bobs here for now


# Submission file - just DOI and comment
flora_with_comments |>
  select(doi_o, comment_text) |>
  write_csv("pubpeer_comments_submission.csv")

cat("Done! Generated", nrow(flora_with_comments), "comments.\n")



```



:::
